justification: All 5 business rules identified with correct invariant classifications (BR-1 through BR-4 as invariant: true, BR-5 weight bracket as invariant: false). State machine directionality explicitly modeled (in_transit → pending forbidden in BR-2, delivered terminal in BR-3). 15 acceptance criteria all in Given/When/Then format. Testable assertions are concrete and developer-actionable. Confidence level of 72 falls within 65-90 range with clearly stated assumptions.

observations.text: All 5 rules correctly identified with accurate invariant classification
State machine directionality explicitly called out — both reverse transition prohibition and terminal state
Weight bracket correctly marked invariant: false with strong justification (management could adjust boundaries without corrupting state)
15 acceptance criteria in clean Given/When/Then format covering happy paths, validation errors, state transitions, and boundary values
Testable assertions are concrete (e.g., '10.01 kg parcel is classified as Heavy') not vague
Confidence at 72 with well-reasoned assumptions and clear separation of high/low confidence areas
Edge cases section identifies bracket boundary ambiguity, Pending→Delivered skip, and floating-point precision — all real implementation risks
Escalations correctly flag the 10 kg boundary ambiguity and missing address validation spec with actionable recommendations
Business impact analysis connects domain rules to concrete stakeholder consequences (revenue leakage, phantom shipments, support tickets)
